description: Symptom Logging & Persistence - Add persistent symptom logging with SymptomContext, wire existing screens to real data, add 30-day follow-up notifications, and gate history behind premium

userStories.title: Create Symptom model and types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Symptom data model so logged symptoms have a consistent shape across the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/models/Symptom.ts with LoggedSymptom interface: id (string), symptomId (string), symptomLabel (string), categoryId (string), categoryLabel (string), affectedPillars (Pillar[]), notes (string), loggedAt (string, ISO), followUpScheduledAt (string | null, ISO), followUpDismissed (boolean), resolved (boolean)
Export LoggedSymptom type from src/models/index.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Pillar type already exists in models/DeviceData.ts. Dates as ISO strings (no Date objects in AsyncStorage). Keep it minimal — just the type definition.

userStories.title: Create SymptomContext with AsyncStorage persistence
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a SymptomContext to persist logged symptoms across app restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/context/SymptomContext.tsx following EquipmentContext pattern
AsyncStorage key: @pearl_symptoms
State: symptoms (LoggedSymptom[]), isLoaded (boolean)
Methods: addSymptom(symptom: LoggedSymptom), resolveSymptom(id: string), dismissFollowUp(id: string), deleteSymptom(id: string), getRecentSymptoms(days: number) returns symptoms within N days
Provider returns null until isLoaded is true
Export SymptomProvider and useSymptoms from src/context/index.ts
Add SymptomProvider to App.tsx provider chain
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow EquipmentContext pattern exactly: createContext → Provider with useState + useEffect for load/save → useCallback mutators → isLoaded gate → named hook. No Date objects — store ISO strings.

userStories.title: Wire SymptomLogModal to save through SymptomContext
userStories.description: As a user, I want my logged symptoms to persist so I can see them when I reopen the app.
acceptanceCriteria: HomeScreen imports useSymptoms from context

handleSymptomLogSave creates a LoggedSymptom object with id (symptomId-timestamp), loggedAt (new Date().toISOString()), followUpScheduledAt (30 days from now as ISO string), followUpDismissed false, resolved false
Calls addSymptom() from context instead of local setState
Remove MOCK_LOGGED_SYMPTOMS constant entirely
Remove local loggedSymptoms useState — read from useSymptoms().symptoms instead
handleDismissSymptom calls deleteSymptom(id) on context
Symptom cards show relative dates computed from loggedAt: Just now (< 1 hour), X hours ago (< 24h), X days ago (< 30d), X weeks ago (30-60d), X months ago (60d+)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Relative date formatting: compute diff between now and loggedAt, return appropriate string. affectedPillars comes from the SymptomLogModal's SYMPTOMS array (look up by symptomId to get affectedPillars, or pass it through the SymptomLogData — may need to extend SymptomLogData to include affectedPillars).

userStories.title: Wire GuidedQuestionsScreen to save answers to OnboardingContext
userStories.description: As a user, I want my onboarding answers to persist so the dashboard can show personalized content.
acceptanceCriteria: In RootNavigator.tsx, the GuidedQuestionsScreenWrapper calls useOnboarding().setAnswers() with the completed answers before navigating to CoachSummary
Answers are saved to OnboardingContext before navigation.navigate('CoachSummary') is called
On app restart, useOnboarding().answers returns the previously saved answers (already works via OnboardingContext AsyncStorage)
HomeScreen coach prompts render based on persisted answers (already works — just needs GuidedQuestions to save)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: OnboardingContext already persists to AsyncStorage and HomeScreen already reads answers. The only gap is GuidedQuestionsScreenWrapper not calling setAnswers(). This is a small edit to RootNavigator.tsx.

userStories.title: Wire SymptomInsightsScreen to read from context
userStories.description: As a user, after logging a symptom I want to see insights based on my logged data.
acceptanceCriteria: SymptomInsightsScreen imports useSymptoms from context
When navigated with specific symptomId, shows details for that symptom from context
When navigated without symptomId, shows summary of all recent symptoms with affected pillars
Shows affected pillars based on the symptom's affectedPillars field from context data
Back button returns to previous screen via navigation.goBack()
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: SymptomInsightsScreen currently uses route params for selected symptoms. Wire it to also read from context for persistence. Keep existing UI structure — just change data source.

userStories.title: Create SymptomFollowUpService for 30-day notifications
userStories.description: As a user, I want Pearl to check in 30 days after I log a symptom to ask if it improved.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/services/SymptomFollowUpService.ts
Export checkSymptomFollowUps(preferences: NotificationPreferences): Promise<PearlNotification[]>
Reads symptoms from AsyncStorage key @pearl_symptoms
For each symptom where followUpScheduledAt is in the past AND followUpDismissed is false AND resolved is false, generate a PearlNotification
Notification category: symptom_followup
Notification title: How's your [symptomLabel]?
Notification body: You logged [symptomLabel] 30 days ago. Has it improved?
Notification data: { screen: 'SymptomInsights', symptomId: symptom.id }
Respects preferences.symptomFollowUp toggle and preferences.enabled master toggle
After generating notification, marks followUpDismissed as true in AsyncStorage to avoid re-sending
Export from src/services/index.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow EquipmentAgingService pattern: read data from AsyncStorage, evaluate conditions, return PearlNotification[]. Mark followUpDismissed=true after generating notification so it fires only once per symptom.

userStories.title: Integrate SymptomFollowUpService into BackgroundSyncService
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the background sync to check for symptom follow-ups.
acceptanceCriteria: Import checkSymptomFollowUps in BackgroundSyncService.ts
Add step after equipment aging check that calls checkSymptomFollowUps(preferences)
Schedule returned notifications via scheduleLocalNotification
Include follow-up notification count in totalNotifs logging
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Small integration: add import, add ~5 lines after the equipment aging block. Pattern identical to how checkEquipmentAging was added.

userStories.title: Premium gate for symptom history
userStories.description: As a free user, I can see symptoms from the last 30 days. Paid users see full history.
acceptanceCriteria: HomeScreen reads @pearl_is_premium from AsyncStorage (default true for demo)
Free users: display symptoms filtered via getRecentSymptoms(30)
Paid users: display all symptoms from context
When free user has older symptoms beyond 30 days, show upgrade banner below the symptom list
Banner text: '[X] older symptoms — Upgrade for full history' with lock-closed-outline icon
Banner styled with accent+10% background, not aggressive
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow same pattern as EquipmentDetailScreen premium gate: useState + useEffect with AsyncStorage.getItem(@pearl_is_premium). Use getRecentSymptoms(30) from context for free tier filtering.
